skillName: Order any integers, negatives and decimals
misconceptionSummary: Students order the absolute values and then attach signs, getting the negative ordering back to front.

lines.text: All already in decimal form — no conversion needed

steps.narration: Write every number in decimal form and pad to equal decimal places. This puts them all in the same format so comparisons are straightforward.
Place all five values on one number line. Negatives fall to the left of zero; positives to the right. Decimals sit between the integer marks.

lines.text: Read left to right for ascending order:

steps.narration: Read the number line from left to right. That gives the ascending order directly — no further calculation needed.

visuals.label: Convert to decimals → place on one line → read left to right

steps.narration: Negative two is the smallest; one is the largest. One number line, left to right, handles any mix of integers, negatives, and decimals.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: ordering absolute values (0.3, 1.7, 2) then reattaching signs — this reverses the negative ordering. Use one number line for the full set.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Don't split into positives and negatives and order separately. Place everything on one number line and read left to right — that handles any mixture correctly.
